title: AutoHeal - User Input Protection: Focusless-First + Focus Theft Recovery
desc: [RULE] Skill content MUST be in English -- Korean uses 2-3x tokens. PHILOSOPHY: focusless-first is the PRIMARY goal. Recovery is safety net only. SELF-IMPROVING: bugs auto-report via suggest, triad dialectic resolves gaps, skill accumulates knowhow. LIVING DOCUMENT: check suggest list periodically.
steps: 0. [RULE] ALL skill steps/desc/tags must be English only -- token efficiency.
1. [PHILOSOPHY] Focusless-first: every CDP action should never need OS foreground. Recovery = safety net. Priority: (1) focusless design, (2) recovery as backstop.
2. [MANDATORY] Restore trigger = end of each ask/CDP action only. Watchdog = monitor+notify only.
3. [MANDATORY] ALL restore paths go through InputReadiness.Probe(SkipBlocker/Knowhow/Zoom=true, AutoApproveYield=true).

4. [FOCUSLESS PATTERN] WARNING: Emulation.setFocusEmulationEnabled(true) is Blink-renderer only -- does NOT prevent OS SetForegroundWindow from Page.bringToFront (path: WebContents::Activate->BrowserFrameViewWin->SetForegroundWindow). Role: auxiliary only (satisfies page-internal JS focus events). SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE(8) is still the primary OS-level prevention. Pattern: (a) visibilityState=visible->skip, (b) SW(8), (c) WaitForWindowStateAsync(minimized, 1000ms), (d) EmulateActiveTabAsync(), (e) CDP call, (f) RestoreChromeNoActivate SW(4), (g) WaitForWindowStateAsync(normal, 1000ms). Degraded: timeout on (c)/(g) -> proceed CDP-only. Chrome M119-121: setWebLifecycleState(active) may intermittently reset visibilityState to hidden. M122+ stable.
5. [DEVICE INSERTION = SELF-REPORTING] Insert AutoBugReport at every theft detection point. OnFocusTheft classifies UNGUARDED vs RACE and files suggest automatically.

6. [SELF-IMPROVING LOOP] Bug detected -> suggest filed -> wkappbot ask triad (share skill + analysis) OR targeted single-AI (ask gemini/claude/gpt) for domain-specific questions -> triad dialectic -> code fix -> skill update -> suggest resolve with evidence. Single-AI useful for: Chrome internals (claude for Blink specifics), JS engine behavior, security model.
7. [OPERATIONAL] Logs: runtime/cdp-focus-risk.jsonl, runtime/cdp-minimize-watchdog.jsonl. Periodic: wkappbot suggest list -> review CDP theft reports -> ask triad if unclear -> fix -> resolve.
8. [INVESTIGATION] When theft: (a) which action, (b) CDP method, (c) IsFocusStealingMethod()?, (d) SW=8?, (e) WaitForWindowState not delay?, (f) OnFocusTheft restores?

9. [DONE] SW(6)->SW(8). BringToFrontAsync WaitForWindowStateAsync. OnFocusTheft ForceForeground+RestoreRetry+AutoBugReport. InputReadiness.Probe. AutoBugReport 5s timeout. EnsureWindowReady ForceForeground. AskGemini prevFg re-capture. DismissDialogAsync consent. EmulateActiveTabAsync in EnsureCdpReadyAsync. InjectFocusLockScriptAsync+SetFocusLockAsync(true/false) around CDP input. Claude+GPT prevFg re-capture + StartFocusWatchdog.
10. [TODO] Scope prevFg per-CDP-run in triad parallel mode (Gap-D). Use AsyncLocal<IntPtr> or per-tab parameter to avoid collision when Gemini/GPT/Claude send concurrently.

11. [JS FOCUS-LOCK] InjectFocusLockScriptAsync() + SetFocusLockAsync(true/false). CRITICAL: do NOT hard-block all focus() -- contenteditable IME (Korean/Japanese) needs focus() for document.activeElement to stay valid during CDP Input.dispatchKeyEvent. Safe pattern: allow focus() only when document.activeElement is body/null (IME recovery case), block all others. window.focus() fully blocked when locked. runImmediately=true covers already-open sandbox tabs. Web Workers: no window/HTMLElement -> unaffected. Isolated Worlds (extensions): not covered.

12. [CONFIRMED BY TRIAD v1.17] (A) setFocusEmulationEnabled is Blink-only; Page.bringToFront always calls SetForegroundWindow regardless -- only solution is not calling bringToFront. (B) Hard focus-lock breaks Korean IME on contenteditable -- fixed to redirect pattern (IME recovery allowed). (C) runImmediately=true covers existing iframes; Web Workers immune. (D) Lifecycle events (window switch, dialog popup) = largest remaining attack surface. (E) ask-flow parity: not all CDP actions have uniform prevFg+watchdog coverage.

13. [TODO] Lifecycle event coverage: window-switch, dialog popup, SPA navigation can steal focus after RestoreChromeNoActivate completes. Need post-restore focus watchdog extension or async-reflow guard.
14. [TODO] Multi-tab prevFg collision in triad mode: AsyncLocal<IntPtr> scoping per concurrent ask-run.

15. [TIPS: TRIAD USAGE PATTERNS] (1) Full triad 정반합: wkappbot ask triad "<question>" -- all 3 AIs answer in parallel, then debate. Best for: architectural decisions, broad gap analysis, design review. Share full skill content for context. (2) Follow-up 정반합: send another triad question on the SAME slack thread (--msg <ts>) after reviewing first round answers. Best for: drilling deeper into unresolved points, validating proposed fixes, testing new code. (3) Targeted single-AI: wkappbot ask claude/gemini/gpt "<question>" -- one AI only. Best for: domain-specific questions (claude for Chrome/Blink internals, gemini for practical patterns, gpt for structured analysis/security). Faster + cheaper than full triad. TIP: Always include project context (C# not Python, Chrome version, specific method names) to avoid Gemini tool-call headscratch. TIP: After triad, send slack reply with summary + your own opinion -- forces synthesis and creates audit trail.
author: claude
